front: What is the primary relationship between UI/UX design and software engineering?
back: They are interconnected disciplines where design decisions directly impact engineering implementation, and technical constraints influence design choices. Successful products require collaboration between both.
The process where designers transfer design specifications to developers, including wireframes, prototypes, and style guides to ensure accurate implementation.
A collection of reusable components, guidelines, and standards that ensure consistency across a product, including colors, typography, components, and patterns.

front: What is the difference between Agile and Waterfall in relation to design?
back: Waterfall is sequential with design completed before development; Agile integrates design iteratively throughout development with sprint-based iterations.
When designers, developers, and stakeholders work together throughout the project lifecycle, sharing responsibilities and knowledge to achieve common goals.
The accumulated cost of shortcuts in design quality that need to be addressed later, similar to technical debt in software development.
Creating quick, interactive mockups of interfaces to test concepts with users and stakeholders before full development begins.

front: What is A/B testing in UI/UX?
back: A method comparing two versions of a page or interface to determine which performs better based on user behavior metrics.
Visual design atoms stored as variables (colors, spacing, fonts) that can be used across design and development tools for consistency.

front: What is the Double Diamond model?
back: A design process framework with four phases: Discover, Define, Develop, and Deliver - alternating between divergent and convergent thinking.
Evaluating a product by testing it with representative users who attempt to complete tasks while researchers observe and collect feedback.

front: What is the difference between low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes?
back: Low-fidelity are simple, paper-based wireframes focusing on layout; high-fidelity are interactive, detailed mockups with visual design ready for user testing.
An approach creating interfaces that adapt to different screen sizes and devices, requiring collaboration between design and engineering.
Building interfaces from reusable, modular components that can be shared between design tools and development frameworks.
